pages_preview.text_preview: HRPO Checklist This document outlines all of the materials that we will need from your lab in order to submit a package for the secondary IRB review by a Department of Defense (DoD) Human Research Protection Official (HRPO). Many of these documents should be created during your local IRB submission process, although some additional documents will be required. We encourage you to consult this list before you begin your local IRB application process, and for sure once you submit to your IRB to ensure that all materials are present and ready for HRPO submission upon local IRB approval. Please do not hesitate to reach out to the SCORE team (scorecoordinator@cos.io) with any questions. Finally, please think carefully about how you name your files when you upload them to OSF. We (SCORE Staff) will have to upload these documents to a web portal by hand, and it is very helpful if each of the bullet points below are in a single, clearly labeled file or series of files (e.g. irb_recruitment_1.pdf; irb_recruitment_2.pdf). ■ IRB application/form submitted to IRB to request review, including all materials enclosed ➢ For some IRBs, this may be an application document that you fill out and send via email or a web portal. For others, it may be an online form that you fill out and submit directly. If that is the case, a PDF of the webform is acceptable. ■ Research protocol (as approved by IRB), including all materials enclosed ➢ Ensure the document version and IRB approval date are clearly marked on your protocol and enclosures. ➢ If a separate protocol from the IRB application exists - if the IRB application and research protocol make up the same document just let us know. ➢ Any study materials or additional documents submitted to and approved by your IRB must also be included. ■ Informed consent document or waiver of consent (as approved by IRB) ➢ Ensure that a footer or stamp is included that clearly states the date of IRB approval date, expiration date, and protocol number. ➢ If applicable, also provide any debriefing documentation/scripts/etc. submitted to and approved by your IRB. ■ Recruitment materials (as approved by IRB) ➢ Any recruitment flyers/emails/posts/etc. submitted to and approved by your IRB must be included. ■ IRB approval/determination letter or exemption memo ➢ If the expiration date is not stated in the letter, please include information that defines the renewal/expiration policy of your IRB. ■ Documentation of human subjects protection training ➢ Although you may combine Completion Reports into a single PDF if you prefer, please keep your PI’s Completion Report as a separate file from that of any other key personnel.

➢ Every person listed on your protocol must provide relevant CITI training certificates/completion reports (or other documentation of human subjects protection training accepted by your institution). ■ Curriculum vitae ➢ A current CV for the PI (and any co-PIs) ➢ If your lab is located outside of the United States: Every person listed as personnel on your protocol will need to provide a current, signed and dated CV. ■ If your protocol is determined to pose greater than minimal risk: Proof of medical monitoring by a party other than the study PI ■ Completed and signed Protocol Submission Form (form 2) ■ Completed Proposal Submission Form (form 8) ■ If your institution is outside of the United States: Completed and signed International Research Study Information Form (form 4)
